title: Business auf DEINE Art
author: Silvia Ladner
description: Du spürst es schon länger: Da ist mehr in dir. Eine Idee, eine Expertise, eine Sehnsucht nach etwas Eigenem. Du hast dich umgeschaut, Kurse gemacht, Strategien probiert und kommst trotzdem nicht dahin wo du spürst, dass es wirklich deins ist. Der Grund dafür hat meist weniger mit Wissen oder Disziplin zu tun, sondern damit, dass der Weg noch nicht wirklich deiner war. Du bist keine naive Träumerin. Du bist eine Macherin, die den richtigen Weg noch nicht gefunden hat. Genau dafür ist dieser Podcast. Willkommen bei Business auf deine Art — dem Podcast für Frauen, die bereit sind daraus ein Business zu machen, das wirklich nach ihnen klingt. Klar positioniert, authentisch geführt, finanziell stabil. Hier sprechen wir über: •	Wie du erkennst welches Business wirklich deins ist •	Positionierung und Marketing, das zu dir passt — nicht zu einer Schablone •	Warum Timing im Business entscheidend ist — und wie du es für dich nutzt •	Business-Klarheit durch deinen inneren Code •	KI richtig eingesetzt — als Hebel wenn du weißt was du aufbauen willst •	Finanzielle Stabilität auf deine Art Ich bin Silvia Ladner. Mit 42 habe ich selbst alles neu aufgebaut — und weiß genau wie sich dieser Punkt anfühlt. Heute verbinde ich 28 Jahre Erfahrung in Marketing und Business-Entwicklung und die Begleitung von über 700 Menschen mit meiner Inner Genius Code™ Methode: jahrtausendealtes Wissen über Persönlichkeit und Energie, übersetzt in handfeste Strategie. Kein Nachmachen, sondern ein Business, das wirklich nach dir klingt. Neue Folgen erscheinen wöchentlich.

title: II Fiote da Lingua
author: A Cafetería
description: Este Fiote da lingua do CPI Viaño Pequeno é o segundo celebrado no centro, mais o primeiro que aparece neste podcast. title: Momentum Unfiltered
author: Tracee Swank and Leon Miles
description: Momentum Unfiltered is the podcast for church leaders who want to face reality with courage, rediscover mission, and find practical next steps for the future. Listen to honest conversations about leadership, discipleship, mission, innovation, and what it takes for churches to stay focused on mission in a changing world.

title: Connection Issues
author: Jacky Minkler, Louis Barry
description: Connection Issues explores the gap between what shapes connection and how we experience it. Hosted by Jacky Minkler and Louis Barry, the show examines the cultural, technological, emotional, and personal forces influencing how we find belonging in modern life. Some episodes explore the systems around us. Others dive into the unseen experiences we carry within us. Together, we're asking why connection feels harder now, and what it takes to rebuild it.

title: By Her Honor with the Honorable Amy Nechtem
author: The Honorable Amy Nechtem
description: A podcast anchored in the conviction that every child has an inherent right to thrive, the show brings together voices from every walk of life, trailblazers in government, business, the arts, and those with lived experience. Through storytelling, honest conversation, and a shared vision, By Her Honor works to build a more compassionate and expansive landscape of opportunity, to truly “change the chance,” and to balance the scales so every child is safe, supported, and seen. Recorded at BevCam Media located in Beverly, Massachusetts Produced by Suffolk in the Hub

title: White Noise | La cara B del albinismo
author: Rubén Romero - @rubenelalbino
description: ¿Cómo es vivir bajo un sol que te quema y las miradas de un mundo que te juzga por desconocimiento? Tengas o no albinismo, bienvenido a la Cara B (B de blanco, sí, había que hacer el chiste). Aquí hablamos claro y sin filtros: olvídate de documentales aburridos. Un espacio de historias reales, anécdotas surrealistas, risas y momentos de mierda. Un proyecto de Rubén Romero (@RubenElAlbino) con charlas entre colegas (cero presiones) y especiales 'Hablando en plata' con expertos. Una mirada humana y diferente a la vida. ¡Dale al play y entra en la Cara B!

description: Hope for the Everyday is a simple, heartfelt podcast where everyday faith meets real life. I’m Troy, sharing stories, reflections, and hope shaped by the faith passed down through my family. Season One walks through Luther’s Small Catechism in a personal, relatable way. Whether you’re Lutheran, Catholic, non‑denominational, or just searching for meaning, you’re welcome here. Each episode ends with a short prayer and a reminder of God’s steady hope .

title: Rise in English
author: Sezgin
description:
Rise in English is a narrated wisdom podcast designed for English learners and deep thinkers who want more than just grammar lessons.





Every episode is a graded English listening practice built around powerful topics — psychology, self-improvement, emotional intelligence, and real-life philosophy. Whether you're commuting, working out, or winding down before sleep, each episode gives you something to think about — in fluent, clear, natural English.





What you'll get:


— Improve your English listening skills naturally


— Explore mindset, psychology, and personal growth topics


— Build vocabulary through real-world context, not textbooks


— Episodes perfect for falling asleep to, focusing, or reflecting





Topics include: emotional intelligence, self-discipline, confidence, the psychology of success, overcoming self-doubt, building wealth habits, and navigating modern life.





New episodes every week. No fluff. Just wisdom — delivered in English worth listening to.





Follow Rise in English and start growing — one episode at a time.

title: Between | by Magpie Bridge
author: Ryan Wong | Magpie Bridge
description: Mỗi tổ chức đều có hai phiên bản. Một phiên bản chính thức. Và một phiên bản thực sự đang xảy ra. Khoảng giữa hai điều đó — mới là nơi sự nghiệp thực sự được định hình. Là nơi những quyết định thực sự được đưa ra. Là nơi những việc quan trọng nhất lặng lẽ xảy ra. dự án Podcasts Between đi vào khoảng cách đó. Những quy tắc không ai viết vào bản mô tả công việc. Những kháng lực ẩn đang tồn tại bên trong tổ chức — trước khi bạn học được cách gọi tên chúng. 每間機構，都有兩個版本。台面上嗰個，同埋實際嗰個。 兩者之間，先係你職場生涯真正成型嘅地方。係決定真正係點做落去嘅地方。係最重要嘅工作，靜靜地發生嘅地方。 Between，探索嗰個距離。嗰啲冇人寫落招聘廣告嘅潛規則。喺你仲未學識叫出佢名之前，已經喺影響你嘅無形力量。  *** Every organisation has two versions of itself — the official one,  and the actual one. Between them is where careers are really shaped, decisions are  really made, and the work that matters actually happens. Between explores that distance: the dynamics nobody puts in the  job description, the invisible forces that move through  organisations before anyone learns to name them. Episodes in Cantonese, Vietnamese, and English. Hosted by Ryan Wong | Magpie Bridge.

title: Raising Kind Minds
author: Liz Walker and Kathryn Lovewell
description:
Raising Kind Minds asks: What sort of voice will your child grow up with?

Because every word they say to themselves begins with what they hear from you.

Hosted by Kathryn Lovewell and Liz Walker of Kind Mind Academy, this podcast helps parents, carers, and educators shape the inner voices of the next generation and strengthen their own along the way. When we nurture a child’s inner voice to be kind, we equip them with the skills to navigate and cope with friendship fires, playground dramas, teenage storms, exam pressures, social media influence, and all the challenges of modern life with confidence and calm.

Each episode blends expert insights, real‑life stories, and practical mindfulness and self‑compassion tools to help adults learn and model healthy self‑talk, soothe their inner critic, and guide children to do the same. Grounded in evidence‑based methods from Mindful Self‑Compassion, positive psychology, and neuroscience, Raising Kind Minds is your invitation to think kinder, feel stronger, and raise children who are happy in their own skin.

title: VIB : Very Important Business
author: Carl Partridge
description:
Very Important Business (VIB) is the weekly podcast giving you the unvarnished truth - from two founders - about what it's really like running a business.


Each week, Carl Partridge (ex-Capital Radio broadcaster, BBC comedy writer, two-time tech founder) and Emily Bendell (founder & CEO of Bluebella) take an honest look at one of the key challenges every founder faces: hiring, firing, raising investment, being a good boss - and, in some cases, a bad one. They're joined by top founders, investors and partners for down-to-earth advice - and they're not shy about calling them out if the advice doesn't ring true.


No humblebragging. No polished LinkedIn takes. Just the stuff that actually happens when you're building a business - the war stories, the mistakes, the things nobody tells you until it's too late.


And they promise to never, ever, take themselves too seriously. Business, without the B.S. Find them at vib.show.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Mobilità Sicura
author: ANCIcomunicare
description:
Mobilità Sicura è il podcast dedicato alla sicurezza stradale dei giovani e alla prevenzione dei comportamenti a rischio alla guida, con particolare attenzione agli incidenti legati al consumo di alcol e sostanze.

Attraverso il contributo di istituzioni, università, polizie locali, esperti scientifici e testimonianze dirette, ogni episodio affronta il tema della guida sicura da un punto di vista diverso: la percezione del rischio, gli effetti di alcol e droghe, il lavoro di prevenzione sui territori, i controlli su strada, le conseguenze degli incidenti e le storie di responsabilità e cambiamento.

Il podcast nasce nell’ambito del progetto nazionale Mobilità Sicura, promosso da ANCI, UPI e dal Dipartimento per le politiche contro la droga e le altre dipendenze della Presidenza del Consiglio dei Ministri, con l’obiettivo di parlare ai giovani in modo diretto, concreto e credibile.

Un racconto corale per ricordare che dietro ogni incidente ci sono scelte, consapevolezza e possibilità di cambiamento. Perché conoscere i rischi può fare la differenza tra mettersi in pericolo e tornare a casa in sicurezza.

title: Emily Brontë (1818 - 1848) - The Visionary
author: Michela Bertazzo
description:
LibriVox volunteers bring you 18 recordings of "The Visionary" by Ellis Bell (Emily Brontë). This was the weekly poem for January 1, 2012.

The first 12 lines originally appeared in one of a large group of Gondal poems, the word coming from the name of a fictitious island kingdom in a fantasy created by Emily and her sister Anne. When Emily finally consented to have some of her poems published in 1846, along with those of sisters Charlotte and Anne, she selected parts of the Gondal poems and removed all reference to the fantasy land. However, this poem first appeared in a new, expanded edition of the sisters' poetry (in 1850, after both Emily and Anne had died) and was apparently derived as follows:

"The Visionary (October 9, 1845)
This poem is part of the same Gondal poem from which Emily carved "The Prisoner. A Fragment." Charlotte Brontë took lines 1-12 of Emily's original poem, "Julian M. and A.G Rochelle," and added 8 lines of her own. Thus, the positive ending in which the watcher has a spiritual experience is Charlotte's and the watcher may be seen as Emily rather than a Gondal character. In Charlotte's version, it is hard to explain the guiding light in the window of stanza 2."

This account is fully supported by other sources. So the poem, as it was published in 1850, is a combination of work by Emily and Charlotte. Charlotte is accused by critics of using a heavy hand in editing some of Emily's formerly unpublished poems for the 1850 volume. (Introduction by Leonard Wilson)

title: פָּרָשַׁת הַשָּׁבוּעַ במבט עסקי - עם מתן צרפתי
author: Matan Tsarfati
description: אני מאמין בכל ליבי שיהדות, רוחניות ועסקים הולכים תמיד יד ביד. ההתפתחות שלנו לא "מפוצלת" היא תמיד כוללת שילובים של לב ושכל, רוח וחומר, פרקטיקה ותודעה. התורה שלנו היא תורת החיים עצמם. בכל פעם שפותחים אותה, נשפך אור חדש על החיים בכל הרמות: פרקטית, עסקית, תודעתית, רגשית, דרך מערכות יחסים. בכל פרק נצלול יחד לפרשת השבוע ונמצא את הקודים הכי עמוקים לפתיחת צינורות השפע, לצמיחה יזמית ולהתפתחות אישית. בפריזמה של כניסה לעולם חדש של עסקים ושיווק, שמתהווה מול עינינו.

author: 金羽留声
description: 《月亮与六便士》这部小说情节并不复杂，写的是一个英国证券交易所的经纪人，本已有牢靠的职业和地位､美满的家庭，但却迷恋上绘画，像“被魔鬼附了体”, 突然弃家出走，到巴黎去追求绘画的理想｡他的行径没有人能够理解｡他在异国不仅肉体受着贫穷和饥饿煎熬，而且为了寻找表现手法，精神亦在忍受痛苦折磨｡经过一番离奇的遭遇后，远遁到与世隔绝的塔希提岛上｡他终于找到灵魂的宁静和适合自己艺术气质的氛围｡他同一个土著女子同居，创作出一幅又一幅使后世震惊的杰作｡在他染上麻风病双目失明之前，曾在自己住房四壁画了一幅表现伊甸园的伟大作品｡但在逝世之前，却命令土著女子在他死后把这幅画作付之一炬｡通过这样一个一心追求艺术､不通人性世故的怪才，毛姆探索了艺术的产生与本质､个性与天才的关系､艺术家与社会的矛盾等等引人深思的问题｡

Through deep narratives, scientific history, and first-principles reasoning, EigenState connects information theory, artificial intelligence, physics, mathematics, robotics, and complex systems into a coherent picture of how reality computes itself. title: Lehrer:innenbildung und Schule im Rassismus
author: GraL & KoNIR
description: Der Podcast „Lehrer:innenbildung und Schule im Rassismus“ ist ein gemeinsames Projekt der Forschungsgruppen ‘GraL’ Gelingensbedingungen rassismussensibler Lehrer:innenbildung und 'KoNIR' Kontinuitäten und Neuformierungen von Institutionellem Rassismus in der Schule. Darin geben die Projekte über einen Zeitraum von acht Folgen Einblicke in ihre Forschungserfahrungen und diskutieren gemeinsam die Bedingungen und Tragweite ihrer Untersuchungen und darüber hinaus. Im Modus des Gesprächs zwischen den Projektmitarbeiter:innen werden Themen wie u.a. das Sprechen über Rassismus, die ‘Normalität’ des Rassismus und die Frage nach dem Zusammenhang zwischen Rassismus & Angst in Schule und Lehrer:innenbildung diskutiert. Auch finden fragende Auseinandersetzung & Vertiefungen mit Phänomenen wie z.B. ‘weiße’ Bildungsräume oder Dimensionen der Betroffenheit von Rassismus statt. Über die Homepages der Forschungsprojekte finden Sie weitere Informationen.

title: De lelijkste flat van Amsterdam
author: Het Parool
description: Aan het Amsterdamse Surinameplein staat het flatgebouw De Klokkenhof, door velen bestempeld als de lelijkste flat van de stad. Het afgelopen jaar haalde de flat de landelijke krantenkoppen toen het gebouw grootschalig werd gekraakt terwijl er nog tientallen vaste huurders woonden. De situatie tussen die bewoners en de krakers liep volledig uit de hand. Journalist Pietro Tramontin fietste er jarenlang langs, maar besluit nu naar binnen te stappen. Hij komt erachter dat de chaos van vandaag wortels heeft in een fascinerend verleden. De flat, die ooit werd gebouwd als een prestigieus en luxueus wooncomplex voor ongehuwde werkende vrouwen, staat inmiddels symbool voor de grillige Amsterdamse woningmarkt. Hij spreekt met angstige bewoners en idealistische krakers over de situatie van nu, en duikt met historici in de glorietijd van het gebouw. Hoe kon De Klokkenhof haar glans zo verliezen? De lelijkste flat van Amsterdam is een podcast van Het Parool.

title: Burnout to Belonging: The home of the Lose Your Mind Podcast
author: Kelly Saward
description:
Burnout is something we often experience but struggle to name. Belonging is something we all want, yet sometimes it feels just out of reach.


Are we losing our minds? Disconnecting from ourselves and each other? Burning out?


We talk a lot about taking the first step. Maybe that step is simply starting the conversation.


That's what we're doing here, mindfully discussing the things that matter, the things that weigh on us, and the things that connect us.


Whatever you're carrying, you're welcome here. We've got you.


Kelly Saward & Alan Muskett have been in conversation for the past five years, exploring the topics that matter most, wellbeing, belonging, burnout, mindfulness, and what it means to live well in an increasingly demanding world.

Both are published authors who have dedicated their work to supporting personal growth and wellbeing. With mindfulness and meditation at the heart of their extensive experience and practice, they bring a wealth of knowledge, insight, and real-world understanding to these conversations.

Through thoughtful dialogue, they create space to explore the challenges we face, the questions we carry, and the practices that can help us reconnect with ourselves and others.


Find out more about Alan Muskett at Right Mindfulness.

Find out more about Kelly Saward at: Room 478 www.kellysaward.com


Together, Kelly and Alan invite you into mindful conversations about the things that feel important, offering reflection, perspective, and practical wisdom for navigating modern life.

title: Divinely Wired with Sarvenaz Elevation
author: Sarvenaz Didar
description:
Welcome to Divinely Wired, a space where listeners come to remember their divinity. Most people spend their lives learning about the world. This podcast is about learning about yourself.

Divinely Wired bridges neuroscience, psychology, spirituality, subconscious programming, nervous system regulation and Kundalini. Not as separate disciplines, but as one unified map of what it means to be human. Because when you truly understand how you're wired, healing stops being something you chase and starts being something you remember.


In a world that profits from disconnection, finding this podcast is already an act of remembrance. Each episode is an invitation to reconnect with the sacred blueprint within, the one coded for abundance, joy, and expansion.


Sarvenaz Didar is a Transformational Therapist and Energy Activation Facilitator who has spent years translating the deepest layers of the human experience, subconscious, nervous system, and energetic, into work that creates change you can actually feel. She didn't find this work. It found her. The hard way.


Divinely Wired is where healing, consciousness, spirituality, and personal development converge, through powerful stories, tools, and modalities designed to bring listeners back to themselves. Because you are not here to merely survive. You are wired to thrive.

title: Fontes de Letras Diferentes
author: Bela
description:
Bem-vindo ao Fontes de Letras Diferentes Podcast, o programa para quem busca fontes de letras para Instagram, letras diferentes, fontes bonitas e ideias criativas para destacar perfis, bios e conteúdos nas redes sociais.

Em cada episódio, exploramos tendências de tipografia, identidade visual, branding digital, marketing para redes sociais e ferramentas que ajudam criadores de conteúdo, influenciadores, gamers e empreendedores a fortalecer sua presença online.

Você vai aprender como usar letras personalizadas, símbolos, fontes estilizadas e estratégias visuais para aumentar o engajamento no Instagram, TikTok, Facebook, YouTube e outras plataformas.
Se você procura inspiração para criar nomes criativos, bios atrativas, textos diferenciados e uma identidade digital marcante, este podcast é para você.

title: Rebrand Land
author: Beth Beeley-Cave | Mindset Coach & NLP Practitioner
description: Rebrand Land is the podcast for six-figure women who are ready to stop performing the next level — and actually become it. You’ve built something real. You have evidence, real evidence, that you’re capable. Six figures through talent, discipline, leadership and refusing to stop. And yet there’s a ceiling that keeps appearing at the same altitude, no matter how many times you change the strategy, the offer, the coach or the content plan. Maybe consistency still feels harder than it should at this stage. Maybe visibility at the scale your business needs still creates a kind of dread your logic can’t explain. Maybe you hit a record month and something always pulls you back to a familiar number. Maybe you’re still doing everything yourself — not because you have to, but because your nervous system genuinely cannot let go. Maybe success feels heavier the bigger it gets, and you can’t work out why. You’ve done the mindset work. You’ve read the books. You know more than enough. So why isn’t it shifting? Because you’ve been working on the surface. And the thing running your income ceiling, your relationship with visibility, your self-sabotage patterns, your leadership and your capacity to receive and hold success… lives in your subconscious programming. Below everything the strategy can touch. Rebrand Land goes there. Hosted by NLP Practitioner, Mindset & Identity Coach for successful women in business and founder of Internal Rebrand, Beth Beeley-Cave, this podcast is rooted in NLP (Neuro-Linguistic Programming), subconscious identity work and the psychology of behavioural change, emotional conditioning and self-concept. This is not generic mindset coaching. This is not manifestation. This is not another framework for a problem that lives below frameworks. Each week we go deep on: → subconscious programming and emotional patterns → identity work and limiting beliefs → nervous system responses and self-sabotage → visibility, leadership and success psychology → the subconscious patterns keeping women in business stuck at the same ceiling → real conversations and identity breakdowns with ambitious women doing this work in real time Because your subconscious mind is not designed to make you successful. It is designed to make you familiar. And once you understand the patterns running underneath your behaviour, success stops feeling like something you’re constantly trying to force… and starts feeling like something you can actually hold. From deep-dive solo episodes to powerful guest conversations, Rebrand Land is where we decode the psychology behind success, self-sabotage, identity shifts and the emotional patterns silently shaping your reality. If you’re ready to build a business and a life at a completely different altitude - welcome to Rebrand Land ✈️ New episodes every week. Connect with us on Instagram @internalrebrand.com or for more ways to work with us, visit www.internalrebrand.com

title: Agnes Mary Frances Robinson (1857 - 1944) - Emily Brontë
author: Michela Bertazzo
description: Emily Brontë (1818-1848) is best known for her only novel, "Wuthering Heights." She was born in Yorkshire, northern England, where her father was an Anglican curate. When Brontë was three years old her mother died of cancer. At the age of six she joined her three sisters briefly at the Clergy Daughters' School, where privations and abuse contributed to the deaths of two of them. Her elder sister, Charlotte, immortalized this terrible place in "Jane Eyre." In 1846 Emily Brontë, under the pseudonym Ellis Bell, published a selection of her poetry. In 1847 appeared her dark, gothic novel, "Wuthering Heights," with its Byronic anti-hero, Heathcliff. Brontë was shy, even reclusive, and never married. In the fall of 1848 she fell ill with inflammation of the lungs, probably due to rapidly-progressive tuberculosis, and died in December of that year, aged twenty-nine. This is a short biography of Brontë written by the British poet, novelist, and critic, Agnes Mary Frances Robinson (1857-1944). (Pamela Nagami)

title: Emily Brontë - The Augustan Books of Modern Poetry:
author: Michela Bertazzo
description:
The inspirational and visionary poetry of Emily Brontë will live forever in the annals of great works of creative art. Renowned as the author of the novel "Wuthering Heights", the mystically contemplative poems of Brontë are featured in this superb collection of some of her best work. These are universally appealing poems that actively examine each and everyone's place in this life of love, loss and wonderment - the wonderment of millions of magical souls on a journey whose terminus remains obscure. Brontë writes of life and exhibits a prescient insight into a mystical universal life form - be it one of a new beginning, a replication, or a heuristic recap of a past long beyond the point of alteration.

We are most fortunate to be able to share the insight of this magnificent poet as she examines the machinations of the collective lived experience and the perception and wisdom of the nature of existence. And since the whim of an ill wind terminated too soon this brilliant soul's worldly reality, we are privileged to be able to learn of her sentiments, her hopes, her disappointments and her innermost feelings in these superb poems of a poet at the peak of prophetic ability and writing prowess.

Yet despite the dire conditions of the life that surrounded her, despite the illness, disappointment and death that was the lived reality of Emily Brontë, she maintained her faith and hope that good will indeed triumph over a seemingly bleak present. In the words of the poet, "I shall sing when night's decay / Ushers in the drearier day."
- Summary by Bruce Kachuk

description:
.🎙️ Episode 0 — The Long Ground & The Deep Blue
Building Back Home with Jackie

In this special introduction episode of Building Back Home with Jackie, I take you back to where it all began.

What started as a lifelong dream — building a home in the country where I was born — slowly became a journey filled with unexpected challenges, emotional moments, red flags, and hard lessons learned along the way.

As a Bajan-born New Yorker with a professional background in Data and Document Control, I believed preparation, organization, and planning would protect the dream. But building from overseas introduced realities I never expected.

In this episode, I share:

🌊 Why building back home meant so much to me
🏠 The 25-year dream behind the journey
📑 What it’s really like managing construction from abroad
🚩 The first warning signs that something wasn’t quite right
⚖️ Why contracts, communication, and documentation matter
🧭 How this experience inspired an educational podcast for the diaspora community

This podcast is not about attacking any individual, company, country, or organization. It is a storytelling and educational platform based on personal experiences, reflections, and lessons learned while navigating an overseas construction journey.

If you’ve ever dreamed of building back home, investing overseas, or creating a legacy for your family — this journey is for you.

Subscribe and follow now on YouTube & Spotify so you don’t miss the official launch.

Real Stories • Red Flags • Lessons Learned

description: LHAP is centered around the Urban Indigenous experience in NYC. We will talk about guests Homelands and How and Why they came to the big city. So join in and enjoy indigenous excellence. Don't worry, everyone is invited, we will also feature some of my favorite Allies!

title: Charlotte Brontë (1816 - 1855) - Shirley
author: Michela Bertazzo
description:
Shirley is an 1849 social novel by the English novelist Charlotte Brontë. It was Brontë's second published novel after Jane Eyre (originally published under Brontë's pseudonym Currer Bell). The novel is set in Yorkshire in the period 1811–1812, during the industrial depression resulting from the Napoleonic Wars and the War of 1812. The novel is set against a backdrop of the Luddite uprisings in the Yorkshire textile industry.
(Summary by Wikipedia)

visto desde la perspectiva del evangelio del agua y el Espíritu   «Pero recibiréis poder, cuando haya venido sobre vosotros el Espíritu Santo, y me seréis testigos en Jerusalén, en toda Judea, en Samaria, y hasta lo último de la tierra». (Hechos 1:8) Los Hechos de los Apóstoles se lee comúnmente como un registro del crecimiento y los milagros de la iglesia primitiva. Sin embargo, el núcleo que la Biblia tiene la intención de decir a través de los Hechos de los Apóstoles no radica solo en testificar de la obra del Espíritu Santo. Cómo el evangelio del agua y el Espíritu ya completado fue testificado a todo el mundo, ese es el mensaje central de los Hechos de los Apóstoles. Este libro explica los Hechos de los Apóstoles desde el capítulo 1 hasta el capítulo 28 desde la perspectiva del evangelio del agua y el Espíritu. Es un registro que sigue cómo fue testificado el evangelio de Jesucristo —quien hizo que se le transfirieran los pecados del mundo al recibir el bautismo de Juan, recibió el castigo de esos pecados en la Cruz, y luego resucitó de entre los muertos. Al leer este libro, llegamos a confirmar cómo el evangelio del agua y el Espíritu dado por el Señor se propaga hasta lo último de la tierra. Además, recibiremos claramente el testimonio de cómo este evangelio salva a los pecadores de los pecados del mundo a través de las dos estructuras del bautismo y la Cruz.  ● Las citas de las Escrituras utilizadas en este libro proceden de la ‘Reina-Valera 1960 (RVR1960)’.  ● Este audiolibro es una obra original creada por humanos, basada en los sermones del Pastor Paul C. Jong. Con el fin de ofrecer una narración clara y una experiencia auditiva cómoda, se utilizó tecnología de IA únicamente para la generación de la voz.

title: 深度解析长尾流量！网友与AI推荐B站专栏阅读，挖掘优质内容的持久生命力
author: 深度解析长尾流量！网友与AI推荐B站专栏阅读，挖掘优质内容的持久生命力
description:

title: The Business of Care
author: CareLineLive
description: The Business of Care is a podcast for people working at the intersection of technology, quality and leadership in health and social care.  Hosted by Marie Page, Head of Marketing at CareLineLive (an all-in-one home care management software platform trusted by over 700 homecare providers across the UK and Australia) each episode brings together experts, innovators and practitioners to explore what it really takes to deliver outstanding care. We get into the data, the tools, the thinking and the decisions that shape how care is delivered on the ground.  This isn't a podcast about software. It's a podcast about the people and ideas that make care work - and the technology that, when it's done right, quietly supports them.  Episodes explore quality benchmarking, AI in care, leadership under pressure, commissioning, workforce, and what the best care organisations are doing differently.  Whether you're running a homecare service, working in an integrated care system, or building the tools that power care delivery - this is your show.  About CareLineLive  CareLineLive is an all-in-one home care management software platform trusted by over 700 homecare agencies and domiciliary care providers across the UK and Australia.   CareLineLive brings together three fully integrated platforms - a management platform for rostering, scheduling, invoicing, payroll and real-time call monitoring; a carer companion app that gives care workers the information they need to deliver person-centred care; and a Care Circle portal that provides families and friends with real-time visibility of visits, notes and medication.  The software also includes MOA Benchmarking, supporting providers in the UK to improve, maintain and evidence their CQC and Care Inspectorate Wales ratings. MOA originates from Australia where it serves more than half of care homes there.   CareLineLive is an NHS England Assured Supplier for Digital Social Care Records, building software that gives carers more time to care. The Business of Care podcast is where that same commitment plays out in conversation, bringing together the experts and innovators shaping what outstanding care looks like in practice.  carelinelive.com

title: Treat Your Health with Care
author: Arron Palmer
description: Treating your health with care means taking conscious steps to maintain and improve your physical, mental, and emotional well-being. It involves eating a balanced diet, exercising regularly, getting enough rest, practicing good hygiene, avoiding harmful substances, and seeking medical attention when necessary. By taking care of your health, you can prevent many diseases, improve your quality of life, and ensure that your body and mind function properly. Good health is essential because it enables individuals to live productive, active, and fulfilling lives.

title: Anne Brontë, Charlotte Brontë and Emily Brontë - Selected Poems by Currer, Ellis and Acton Bell
author: Michela Bertazzo
description:
Poems by Currer, Ellis and Acton Bell was a volume of poetry published jointly by the three Bronte sisters, Charlotte, Emily and Anne in 1846, and their first work to ever go in print. To evade contemporary prejudice against female writers, the Bronte sisters adopted androgynous first names. Marked by profound sentiments, gravity and melodious harmony, the poems are strewn on the fields of soulful love, rueful reminiscence and the immortal yearnings of a Christian soul, and represent a fragrant assemblage of noetic flowers from the glebes of olden England. For those not familiar with the Bronte sisters' poetry, it should be noted that many of their poems were written in the context of their fictional, shared worlds of Gondal and Angria.
(Written by Ellis Christoff)

title: Humanitea
author: John Britton & Angela Jamieson
description:
Get yourself a cup of tea and join us.

Angela is a Chinese-Canadian engineer who’s worked around the world and now publishes books and shares her approach to ‘Relaxed Productivity’.

John is a Welsh artist who now lives in Ireland, after 30 years as a performer, director, improvisor and teacher. He specialises in creativity, overcoming anxiety and developing presence & focus.

‘Humanitea’ is conversations between the two of us, and sometimes invited guests. We talk about keeping it real in an age of bullshit and bluster. Though we've not much in common, we've discovered we’ve got a hell of a lot in common.

We talk about keeping it authentic and honest, as we navigate the complexity and overwhelm of the modern world.

Together we set up School of Human Kind (www.schoolofhumankind.com) - an international network of coaches, thinkers, teachers, writers and others assorted humans.

We believe totally in the power of conversation, connection and kindness.

Come join us as we explore how to keep all three alive in this crazy world.

title: Charlotte Brontë (1816 - 1855) - Jane Eyre: An Autobiography
author: Michela Bertazzo
description:
Jane Eyre is an extraordinary orphan girl who is treated horribly by her aunt Mrs. Reed and her children. She is sent away to a girl’s boarding school called Lowood. She studies there for a total of six years and graduates to teach at the same school for two years. After two years of teaching, she leaves Lowood to go looking for another job, at 19. She meets Mrs. Fairfax who decides to give her the job as a governess at Thornfield Hall. She eventually falls in love with Mr. Rochester, the master of Thornfield. She is happy being a governess to Adele and being in love with Mr. Rochester. Mr. Rochester then decides to propose to her: However, Mr. Rochester has a deep dark secret; one that stops the marriage and restricts them from being together forever. She runs away and lives a new life with a new family, St. John and his two sisters. St. John finds out that Jane Eyre is a beneficiary of her uncle’s wealth and insists that she marry him. She refuses because she does not love him and runs away. She goes back to Thornfield only to find that Mr. Rochester’s circumstances have changed for the worse.
Summary - bartleby.com

author: Michela Bertazzo
description:
LibriVox volunteers bring you 16 recordings of Evening Solace by Charlotte Brontë.
This was the Fortnightly Poetry project for March 24, 2024.
------
Charlotte Brontë was an English novelist and poet, the eldest of the three Brontë sisters who survived into adulthood and whose novels became classics of English literature.
This poem is taken from Poems by Currer, Ellis, and Acton Bell. To evade contemporary prejudice against female writers, the Brontë sisters adopted masculine first names. All three retained the first letter of their first names: Charlotte became Currer Bell, Emily became Ellis Bell, and Anne became Acton Bell. - Summary by Wikipedia

description: Twenty years ago, now, I attempted (but was not especially successful in the task) to establish upon the personal knowledge that my own residence as a pupil in the historical Pensionnat in the Rue d'Isabelle, at Bruxelles gave me of the facts of Charlotte Brontë's relationships to Monsieur and Madame Heger, right impressions about the experiences and emotions she underwent between 1842 and 1846, and that supply the key and clue to the right interpretation of her genius. Every opinion I then ventured to state, not upon the authority of any special power of divination or of psychological insight of my own, but solely upon the authority of this personal knowledge of Monsieur and Madame Heger in my early girlhood, and also of the information I owed to the friendship and kind assistance given me, in my endeavour to rectify false judgments, by the Heger family, has quite recently, not only been confirmed, but established upon entirely incontrovertible evidence, by the generous gift made to English readers throughout the world of the key needed to unlock once and for ever the tragical but romantic 'Secret' of Charlotte Brontë. - Summary by Frederika MacDonald

title: TAP | Digitale, cultura e altre complicazioni
author: Scuola nazionale del patrimonio e delle attività culturali
description:
Cosa succede davvero quando il digitale entra in un museo, un archivio, una biblioteca, un parco archeologico?
Non solo nuovi strumenti, ma scelte difficili: cosa rendere accessibile, a chi parlare, come raccontare memorie complesse senza appiattirle, come evitare che i dati si trasformino in burocrazia.

TAP | Digitale, cultura e altre complicazioni è il podcast della Scuola nazionale del patrimonio e delle attività culturali curato da Francesco Mannino (Officine Culturali di Catania) che indaga le pratiche, gli strumenti e le trasformazioni digitali nei luoghi della cultura.

La serie parte da TAP | Tutoring e Accompagnamento Progetti - l'iniziativa che all'interno di Dicolab. Cultura al digitale ha accompagnato diciannove enti culturali nello sviluppo di progetti digitali - ma va oltre TAP, per raccontare il lavoro culturale nel momento in cui prova a cambiare: esitazioni, scelte, attriti, competenze che mancano e che si costruiscono.
Non il racconto di singole esperienze, ma la restituzione di processi in corso che mettono in luce - e in comune - motivazioni, scelte, criticità e apprendimenti emersi lungo la strada.

Cinque episodi, cinque traiettorie di cambiamento, che raccolgono all'interno di una domanda comune le voci di chi questi progetti li ha pensati e portati avanti: che cosa cambia, nel lavoro culturale, quando il digitale finisce di essere una parola comoda e comincia a incidere su come si lavora, si comunica, si decide?

Il digitale, quando funziona, non rende semplicemente tutto più semplice, veloce, pratico e utile. 
Fa venire a galla ciò che era già lì e faticava ad emergere: relazioni fragili, patrimoni difficili, pubblici poco conosciuti, competenze da costruire, decisioni da prendere. 
E richiede di rallentare, di confrontarsi con la complessità, con le difficoltà e con l’eredità del passato.
Ed è proprio da lì che conviene cominciare ad ascoltare.
itunes_owner_name: Scuola nazionale del patrimonio e delle attività culturali

This technology is particularly valuable for hospitals, laboratories, pharmacies, restaurants, warehouses, and manufacturing facilities where temperature fluctuations can lead to product loss or compliance issues. Industry observers note that advanced Remote Temperature Monitoring systems also simplify regulatory reporting through automated data logging and comprehensive recordkeeping. Solutions such as those offered by TempGenius provide continuous visibility into critical environments, helping organizations improve accountability, reduce manual workload, and protect valuable assets. title: The Heart of Fertility
author: Sam Reynolds
description:
Welcome to The Heart of Fertility.... a gentle, honest and emotionally supportive space for women carrying the invisible weight of fertility struggles, overwhelm and trying to hold everything together.


Hosted by fertility doula, former midwife and founder of The Fertility Sanctuary, Sam Reynolds, this podcast explores the emotional reality behind fertility journeys and the deep connection between mind, body and emotional wellbeing.

Each week you’ll find two short, heartfelt episodes designed to help you feel less alone, more grounded and emotionally supported through the highs, lows and uncertainty of trying to conceive and beyond.


Together, we’ll explore:


  • the emotional impact of fertility struggles
  • nervous system support and emotional wellbeing
  • mindset, mindfulness and meditation
  • coping with overwhelm, anxiety and grief
  • relationships, identity and resilience
  • holistic and compassionate approaches to support
  • honest conversations around healing, hope and being human


Whether you’re trying to conceive naturally, exploring IVF or fertility treatment, coping with loss, or simply feeling emotionally exhausted by the pressure of it all, this is a space where you don’t have to pretend you’re okay.


Because fertility affects so much more than just the body.


Subscribe now and visit The Fertility Sanctuary for additional support, sanctuary experiences, resources and inspiration.

Qui influence vraiment les décisions politiques ?
Comment les médias choisissent-ils les sujets dont tout le monde parle ?

title: The Wealth We Build
author: Center for Community Wealth
description:
The old economy is broken, and a new one isn't going to build itself. If we want an economy that actually works for all, we have to BUILD it.


Welcome to your weekly blueprint for economic liberation. Produced by the Center for Community Wealth (CCW), this podcast spotlights the fearless people, high-stakes projects, and bold ideas reshaping what’s possible for Metro Denver and beyond. We’re dismantling the old system and building a resilient, self-determined future from the ground up.


Each week, we dive into one of four pillars fostering real community wealth: cooperatives, small businesses, anchor institutions, and community real estate. No gatekeepers, no trickle-down myths, just raw, inspiring conversations with the entrepreneurs, worker-owners, and thought leaders who are actively creating economic justice with solidarity, sovereignty and joy.


Stop waiting for the system to change. Tune in and learn how we're building it. Join us!

This podcast takes a curious approach to the questions that matter most: Why do we feel stuck? How do our identities take shape? What does it mean to live a meaningful life? This isn't a podcast about easy answers. It's a podcast about asking better questions. Because life is complicated. Let's keep it that way.

title: Dancing With Destiny - Beyond The Highlight Reel
author: Vicky Galbraith
description: Dancing With Destiny is a podcast exploring the real stories behind the highlight reels. Hosted by Vicky Galbraith, this is a space for honest, thoughtful and sometimes vulnerable conversations with guests from all walks of life — sharing the twists, challenges, setbacks, lessons and unexpected moments that shaped their journey. Because life rarely unfolds in a perfectly filtered straight line. Behind every business, personal transformation or “success story” is usually a far more human reality filled with uncertainty, resilience, growth, wobbles and moments nobody else saw. This podcast exists to gently explore those conversations. The parts we often hide. The lessons we learn quietly. The moments that changed everything. And the reminder that none of us are figuring life out as perfectly as it sometimes appears online. So settle in and join us as we look beyond the highlight reel together.

title: 弄得（飲んで）/ 人森短短起個秋
author: 處理姐、牛牛
description: 【Season 2】弄得（飲んで） 喝一點，就開始「弄得」。 《弄得（飲んで）》由兩個酒杯永遠見底的好友─牛牛與處理姐主持。 我們不談大道理，只聊那些酒後敢講、醒著不敢說的真心話。從戀愛關係的荒唐瞬間，到職場八卦、性愛迷思、孤單乾杯的夜晚，每一集都像是深夜的微醺告解室。有時爆笑、有時心酸，更多時候─你會在我們的醉話裡，聽見自己的心聲。 來吧，倒滿一杯，喝進真心，鬥陣來吐出人性。 【Season 1】人森短短起個秋 由三位資深電視媒體製作人共同主持，他們以50+的人生歷練，在美食與旅遊節目領域擁有豐富經驗。 處理姐以敏銳的文化洞察和對電影、流行音樂的深入見解著稱；牛牛擅長掌控節目流程，尤其擁有豐富的旅遊節目製作經歷；而Ricky則在視覺藝術和大型演唱會製作方面展現了極高的專業水準。 透過他們獨特的個人經歷和深厚的專業背景，節目不僅帶來有趣的話題討論，還充滿了對美食、旅遊、流行文化的真知灼見，讓聽眾一同探索最生活化卻最難忘的故事。 -- Hosting provided by SoundOn

title: Ιστορίες Ελπίδας
author: pod.gr
description: Ένας στους πέντε ανθρώπους θα αναπτύξει καρκίνο σε κάποια στιγμή της ζωής του. Αυτή η στιγμή μπορεί να έρθει στην ώριμη, στη μέση ηλικία, στα νεανικά του χρόνια, ενώ είναι παιδί ή πριν καν γεννηθεί. Το Σωματείο Ελπίδα- Σύλλογος Φίλων Παιδιών με καρκίνο, ιδρύθηκε το 1990 από μια ομάδα δυναμικών γυναικών με Πρόεδρο την Μαριάννα Βαρδινογιάννη. Μέχρι τις αρχές της δεκαετίας του 90 στην Ελλάδα δεν υπήρχε μονάδα μεταμόσχευσης μυελού των οστών για τα παιδιά που έπασχαν από λευχαιμία. Το έργο της Ελπίδας ξεκίνησε από τη δημιουργία της πρώτης, αμιγώς παιδιατρικής μονάδας γι΄αυτό το σκοπό και εξελίχθηκε στη δημιουργία του πρώτου ογκολογικού νοσοκομείου για παιδιά. Η ανάγκη των παιδιών για φιλοξενία, οδήγησε τη Μαριάννα Βαρδινογιάννη και στη δημιουργία του Ξενώνα της Ελπίδας ενώ λίγα χρόνια αργότερα ο Σύλλογος Όραμα Ελπίδας ιδρύθηκε για να αποτελέσει έναν ακόμη κρίκο στην αλυσίδα της προσφοράς για τα παιδιά που πάσχουν από σοβαρά νοσήματα αλλά και τους ενήλικες που χρειάζονται μεταμόσχευση μυελού των οστών και αναζητούν μόσχευμα. Σ΄αυτό το podcast η Αθηναΐς Νέγκα καταγράφει μερικές από τις ιστορίες των παιδιών που κάποτε αρρώστησαν και σήμερα είναι υγιείς ενήλικες, των οικογενειών τους, των γιατρών, του νοσηλευτικού προσωπικού αλλά και των δοτών που σώζουν ζωές δωρίζοντας μυελό των οστών.

title: NBI Clearance Renewal and Application Help Philippines
author: Carlo Reyes
description:
Getting an NBI Clearance is not always simple, especially when you are trying to understand renewal rules, online application steps, appointment booking, or the documents you need before visiting a branch. Many people search for answers because they do not want to waste time, miss a requirement, or make a mistake during the process. This podcast is built to solve those problems in a simple and practical way.

What This Podcast Helps You With

This podcast focuses on the real questions people ask about NBI Clearance in the Philippines. Some listeners want to know how NBI Clearance renewal works, while others need help with first-time application steps, appointment booking, valid ID requirements, or common clearance issues. Instead of using complicated explanations, the show breaks each topic into simple guidance that is easier to follow.

Understand NBI Clearance Renewal and Application Steps

Many applicants do not know whether they should renew online, book a new appointment, or prepare extra documents before starting the process. This podcast explains the difference between renewal and application, what steps usually come first, and what details you should check before moving forward.

Learn What to Prepare Before Your Appointment

A common problem is reaching the appointment stage without knowing what to bring. Episodes in this podcast help listeners understand the role of valid IDs, payment confirmation, appointment details, and other practical requirements that may affect the NBI Clearance process.

Get Help With Common NBI Clearance Problems

Some applicants face delays because of incorrect details, confusion about the online system, or uncertainty about what happens after registration. This podcast covers those common concerns and gives listeners a clearer idea of how to approach the process with fewer mistakes.

FAQs

Who is this podcast for?

This podcast is for listeners in the Philippines who want simple help with NBI Clearance, including first-time applicants, people looking for NBI Clearance renewal guidance, and anyone trying to understand appointments, requirements, or common process questions.

Will this podcast only talk about NBI Clearance renewal?

No. While NBI Clearance renewal is one of the main topics, the podcast also covers online application steps, appointment booking, requirements, valid IDs, and practical tips that can help both new and returning applicants.

What kind of problems will the podcast explain?

The podcast covers issues such as how to start the process, what documents to prepare, how appointment booking works, what to expect after online registration, and how to avoid common mistakes that slow down the NBI Clearance process.

Conclusion

If you want a practical podcast that explains NBI Clearance in a clear and useful way, this show is designed to help. It focuses on NBI Clearance renewal, application steps, appointment guidance, and common questions so listeners can understand the process better and prepare with more confidence.

description: Noos Media est le podcast qui analyse les systèmes qui nous enferment : travail, famille, école, santé mentale, argent, institutions, relations et numérique. Chaque épisode part d’un problème concret : une décision qui tourne en rond, une émotion qui persiste, une organisation qui se bloque, une relation qui se répète, une société qui réagit toujours au mauvais endroit, pour identifier ce qui maintient réellement la situation. Ici, on ne cherche pas seulement les causes visibles. On observe les boucles, les interactions, les contradictions, les tentatives de solution qui aggravent le problème, les équilibres cachés qui empêchent le changement. Inspiré par l’approche systémique de Palo Alto, la cybernétique, la pensée complexe et l’analyse des organisations, Noos Media propose une lecture exigeante des blocages humains et collectifs. Pourquoi certains problèmes se répètent-ils alors que tout le monde veut les résoudre ? Pourquoi certaines décisions échouent-elles malgré les bonnes intentions ? Pourquoi le contrôle, la communication, la motivation ou la réforme produisent-ils parfois l’inverse de ce qu’ils promettent ? Noos Media s’adresse aux dirigeants, DRH, managers, consultants, thérapeutes, enseignants, décideurs, mais aussi à tous ceux qui veulent comprendre les mécanismes invisibles qui organisent nos comportements. Chaque épisode aide à voir ce qui bloque vraiment, au lieu d’intervenir une fois de plus au mauvais endroit. Noos Media - Comprendre les systèmes qui nous enferment - https://noos.media Hébergé par Ausha. Visitez ausha.co/fr/politique-de-confidentialite pour plus d'informations.

title: Free EMDR Therapy
author: Free
description:
Want EMDR‑style therapy without the price tag? Free EMDR Therapy gives you powerful, guided sessions for exactly that.

Just grab your headphones or buds, find a safe, private space, and press play.

You can do each session as many times as feels right for you.

Inside you’ll get:

  • EMDR‑inspired audio sessions that use bilateral stimulation to help your brain process stress, old stuff, and emotional charge

  • NLP‑flavoured change work, in the spirit of the big stage coaches, but paired with bilateral sound to trigger an EMDR‑like response

  • Calming and integration tracks to help you settle your nervous system after deeper work

This is intense inner work, not background noise. Don’t listen while driving, working, or operating machinery. It’s a self‑help tool, not a substitute for a qualified therapist or crisis service. If things get too much, hit pause, ground yourself, or reach out for support.

title: Krankenhausführung neu denken
author: Jörg Gottschalk
description: Der größte Teil der Arbeit im Krankenhaus wird nicht für Patienten geleistet, sondern fließt in die Beherrschung der eigenen chaotischen Organisation. Mitarbeitende haben nicht zu wenig Zeit, sondern sie müssen sich zu viel Arbeit machen. Krankenhausführung neu denken ist ein Podcast für alle, die Krankenhäuser wirksamer, menschlicher und wirtschaftlich erfolgreicher gestalten wollen. Die zentrale These: Die Überlastung in Krankenhäusern entsteht nicht nur durch Personalmangel oder fehlende Finanzierung. Ein erheblicher Teil der Belastung wird täglich durch organisatorisches Chaos, instabile Abläufe und vermeidbare Verschwendung erzeugt. Im Mittelpunkt stehen daher drei entscheidende Fragen: • Wie lassen sich Behandlungsprozesse stabil, effizient und patientenorientiert gestalten? • Wie können Mitarbeitende selbst zu Treibern kontinuierlicher Verbesserung werden? • Welche Rolle muss Führung übernehmen, damit Veränderung nachhaltig gelingt? Der Podcast zeigt, wie Krankenhäuser durch strukturierte Prozesse, Teamboarding, kontinuierliche Verbesserung und wirksame Führung Qualität steigern, Mitarbeitende entlasten und wirtschaftlich erfolgreicher werden können. Für Führungskräfte, Ärztinnen und Ärzte, Pflegende und alle, die überzeugt sind: Die Zukunft des Krankenhauses entscheidet sich nicht zuerst in der Politik, sondern in den Prozessen, Teams und Führungssystemen der eigenen Organisation.

title: Legal Forward
author: Claudia Jandek, Jason Lau-Christen
description:
Der Rechtsmarkt steht Kopf.

Welche juristischen Berufe es in zehn Jahren noch gibt, weiß heute niemand. Wer jetzt studiert oder im Referendariat sitzt, bereitet sich auf einen Beruf vor, der sich gerade selbst neu erfindet.

Bei Legal Forward sprechen wir mit den Menschen, die diese Transformation aktiv gestalten. Wir fragen, wie sich Geschäftsmodelle verschieben, welche Skills zählen werden — und was das konkret für dich bedeutet.

Claudia Jandek, Volljuristin und Autorin, und Jason Lau-Christen, Legal AI Solutions Architect und Lecturer — beide arbeiten täglich an der Schnittstelle von Recht und KI.

description: 「わたしの気持ちを軽くする　こころのはなし」は、 日常の中で感じる不安やモヤモヤ、人間関係の悩みについて、やさしく言葉にしていくPodcastです。 私はカウンセラーをしていますが、私自身に悩みがないわけでも、いつも心が整っているわけでもありません。 人にアドバイスをしていても、自分自身はうまく出来ていないこともたくさんあります。 この番組では、“専門家として正しい答えを伝える”というのではなく、同じように悩みながら生きるひとりとして、みなさんと一緒に心を整理していけたらと思っています。 聴き終わる頃に、少しだけ気持ちが軽くなる。 そんな時間をお届けできたら嬉しいです。

title: Inside the Reading Gap
author: Thinking Reading
description:
Millions leave school unable to read properly. This four-part podcast uncovers the hidden literacy crisis, its human cost, and the solutions that can change lives.

This problem has inflicted incalculable harm and blighted lives with misery. The worst part is that this is unnecessary – we have the knowledge we need to teach all children and young people to read. The solutions are far cheaper than the ongoing costs of illiteracy. 

In this four-part podcast, you will hear from headteachers and principals, teachers, charity leaders, researchers and academics, literacy teaching specialists, and most importantly, young people and adults who have struggled with their reading. We tell the story of their pain and frustration, and the incredible power that learning to read confers when they finally get the help that they need.

title: Awakening: The Psychic Odyssey
author: Andrea Allen
description: What if your nightmares were instructions? Psychic Amelia awakens in an inhuman labyrinth with other captives. Their "evolution" is a lie; they are the sacrifice for Project Ladder Rung. To escape the walls that whisper betrayal, they must master their trauma. But the door they seek to open might unleash a cosmic horror best left sealed—the Architects are waiting. The Awakening: Psychic Odyssey - A psychological thriller blending cosmic horror and paranoid tension. Subscribe now to descend into the labyrinth.

title: Between Visits
author: Insight Health
description: Between Visits What happens between the visits is what makes the visits work. Most of medicine doesn't happen in the exam room. It happens in the workflows around it: the staff huddle, the prior auth, the callback, the scheduling block, the follow-up that gets missed when no one's watching the queue. That's where a practice actually lives or dies, and it's where the people doing the work rarely get to talk shop in public. Between Visits is a conversation with the clinicians and operators who run those moments. Independent physicians who built their own practices. Administrators who quietly hold a clinic together. Founders who left a big system to build something different. Leaders trying to make medicine work for both patients and the people caring for them. We skip the buzzwords. We dig into the actual work: what got hard, what got broken, what got rebuilt, and what they wish someone had told them on day one. Hosted by physician-founders Dr. Eric Stecker (cardiology), Dr. Pankaj Gore (neurosurgery), Saran Siva and Jaimal Soni, co-founders of Insight Health. New episodes when we have a guest worth listening to. For clinicians, practice operators, and anyone curious about how care actually gets delivered.

title: What They Left Behind:  The Coates Brief
author: Tracey J. Coates
description:
Prince had $156 million and no will. Aretha Franklin's last wishes were found in a spiral notebook under a couch cushion. Chadwick Boseman knew he was dying for four years and never wrote a single document protecting what he built.

These are not stories about careless people. They are stories about brilliant, accomplished people who put off the one thing that would have protected everyone they loved.

Every Tuesday, estate planning and family law attorney Tracey J. Coates breaks down what actually happened, what the law did with it, and what you can do right now so your family is not the next cautionary tale.

What They Left Behind: The Coates Brief is the estate planning conversation you have been meaning to have.

Licensed in Maryland and Washington, D.C. New episodes every Tuesday at coatesfamilylaw.com.

title: Matka et moi
author: Mathilde bourdon
description: Le mot "wellness" est partout. Tellement partout qu'il a fini par ne plus rien dire. Matka, c'est le podcast qui lui rend sa profondeur  et qui te donne les vraies clés pour trouver ta voie et les techniques pour aller mieux. Chaque semaine, je reçois une femme ou un homme dont le métier est d'accompagner les autres vers le mieux-être : thérapeute, coach, sophrologue, naturopathe, praticien·ne en médecines douces, professeur·e de yoga, hypnothérapeute, psy, travailleur·se du soin… Des personnes qui, loin des promesses faciles, ont fait du bien-être un véritable savoir-faire. Ensemble, on parle de leur parcours souvent une reconversion, parfois une vocation tardivedes outils concrets qu'iels utilisent au quotidien, des techniques qu'iels enseignent, et de ce que leur pratique leur a appris sur elles et eux-mêmes. Mon objectif : que tu repartes de chaque épisode avec une clé en plus  une pratique, une question, une piste pour avancer dans ta propre quête. Te former, te comprendre, te trouver. Et apprendre, peu à peu, à aller mieux. Matka, c'est "mère" en polonais. Une figure qui prend soin, qui transmet, qui fait grandir. C'est cette énergie-là que j'ai voulu mettre au cœur du podcast.  Abonne-toi pour ne rien manquer. Hébergé par Ausha. Visitez ausha.co/fr/politique-de-confidentialite pour plus d'informations.

title: A History of Iniquity
author: Brevity Studios
description:
A History of Iniquity is a history-and-true-crime podcast about real crimes from the deep past — and the civilisations that produced them. Each episode, host Ryan Wolf takes a single documented crime from the ancient or pre-modern world — a murder, a conspiracy, a betrayal, a trial — and uses it as a doorway into the world it happened in: the politics, the daily life, the beliefs, and above all the law.


Because you can't understand the crime until you understand the world. A trained lawyer with a lifelong love of history, Ryan is fascinated by both halves — the world-building and the justice — and brings you along as he goes, with the genuine enthusiasm of someone learning alongside you.


Expect rigorously researched, immersive storytelling, and at least a few "I had no idea" moments every episode, in both directions. Season One travels the ancient world from oldest to newest — Egypt, Assyria, Rome, the Norse world, feudal Japan and beyond. One civilisation, one crime, at a time.

title: 8-Figure ROI Body & Mind: Transformational Health & Wellness Coaching for Founders, Business Owners, CEOs, and Executives [50 Client Success Stories]
author: Jeffrey Tan
description: For business owners, CEOs, founders, executives, and entrepreneurs looking to transform their health and wellness, which often leads to large increases in revenue, profit, and ROI.  Created and hosted by Jeffrey Tan, a high-performance transformation coach who has helped over 140 top performers transform their health, often resulting in their financial bottom lines increasing by multiple eight figures or more.  Stop treating your health as an afterthought.   Start treating you health as a strategic business advantage.    This podcast bridges the gap between peak human biology and exponential financial growth.  This podcast dissects the exact nutritional protocols, advanced supplementation, mindset shifts, and lifestyle architecture that Jeffrey has executed with his high-performing coaching clients to completely transform their health and wellness, which leads to massive transformations of their financial bottom line as well.   You will learn how you too can transform your health and wellness, which can often lead to a massively increased revenue, profit, and ROI.  If you are ready to upgrade your biology to scale your success in all areas of life, this is your blueprint.  In today’s hyper-competitive corporate landscape, your physical and mental vitality are your most critical assets. Yet, too many high-level leaders sacrifice their well-being on the altar of hustle culture. You push through brain fog with caffeine, ignore chronic pain to close deals, and let stress dictate your sleep schedule. What is the actual cost of a compromised biological system? It is the missed detail in a multi-million-dollar contract, the explosive temper that alienates your top talent, and the chronic fatigue that stifles your company’s scalability.  This show is designed exclusively for the ambitious professional who understands that elite business performance is impossible without elite physical health. We dive deep into the science of biohacking, cognitive enhancement, and executive wellness to provide actionable, data-driven strategies you can implement immediately. Whether you are scaling a startup or leading a global enterprise, optimizing your biology is the ultimate leverage point for explosive business growth.  This serves as a masterclass in high-performance habits, exploring the profound connection between a resilient body, a razor-sharp mind, and a booming balance sheet. We cover comprehensive topics tailored to the demanding lifestyle of a modern executive. Expect deep dives into eradicating entrepreneurial burnout, mastering stress management and cortisol regulation, reversing chronic inflammation, and unlocking sustained, jitter-free energy. We explore the gut-brain axis to maximize mental clarity, detail dietary frameworks to combat metabolic syndrome, and outline advanced supplementation stacks designed to repair the cellular damage caused by corporate stress.  As a founder, you meticulously track your KPIs, conversion rates, and profit margins. It is time to apply that same rigorous, analytical approach to your physiology. By tuning your body like a high-performance machine, you directly enhance your executive presence, emotional intelligence, and strategic decision-making capabilities. Optimizing your sleep architecture directly correlates with enhanced problem-solving skills, and stabilizing your blood sugar can be the difference between a panicked negotiation and a masterful closing.  We deliver clinical insights, proven biological frameworks, and real-world case studies of executives who reclaimed their vitality and subsequently multiplied their company valuations. You will discover how prioritizing your physical health translates directly into measurable corporate success.  Learn how to biohack your way to the top and engineer a body capable of sustaining massive, long-term success.

title: EigenState
author: Shinde Aditya
description: EigenState is a long-form science and technology podcast exploring the physical foundations of intelligence, computation, mathematics, and emerging technologies. Each series investigates a fundamental question shaping the future of civilization: Why does intelligence require energy? Why does AI live inside geometry? What are the limits of computation? And what happens when physics becomes the bottleneck? Through deep narratives, scientific history, and first-principles reasoning, EigenState connects information theory, artificial intelligence, physics, mathematics, robotics, and complex systems into a coherent picture of how reality computes itself. Hosted by Shinde Aditya.

title: Mi esposa me fue infiel y me lo contó
author: Jessi leonardo Cuevas ruiz
description:
Nunca imaginé que una noche común cambiaría mi vida para siempre. Ella llegó a casa más callada de lo normal. Sus ojos estaban rojos y evitaba mirarme. Pensé que había tenido un mal día, pero cuando se sentó frente a mí y comenzó a llorar, sentí que algo no estaba bien.

—Necesito decirte algo —susurró con la voz temblorosa.

El silencio se hizo eterno. Entonces me confesó que me había sido infiel. Cada palabra cayó sobre mí como un golpe. Sentí que el tiempo se detenía mientras intentaba comprender lo que estaba escuchando. Ella lloraba desconsoladamente, repitiendo que estaba arrepentida y que había decidido contarme la verdad porque ya no soportaba vivir con la culpa.

Yo no sabía qué decir. La mujer que había amado durante años estaba frente a mí, rota por sus decisiones, mientras yo trataba de recoger los pedazos de la confianza que acababa de perder. Aquella noche hablamos hasta el amanecer. Hubo lágrimas, preguntas sin respuesta y un dolor difícil de describir.

Al final entendí que algunas confesiones liberan a quien las hace, pero dejan una tormenta en el corazón de quien las escucha. Y mientras el sol comenzaba a salir por la ventana, comprendí que mi vida jamás volvería a ser la misma.

title: Why Businesses Can't Ignore Data Security Anymore
author: SoftPro
description:
Welcome to the SoftPro Podcast https://soft-pro.pl/, where we discuss software development, business automation, cloud technologies, and the challenges companies face as they modernize their operations.

Today, we're talking about a topic that affects businesses of every size - why data security can no longer be treated as an afterthought.

As companies become more digital, they rely on software to manage customer information, financial records, internal processes, and day-to-day operations. From cloud platforms and web applications to internal business systems, organizations are handling more data than ever before.

And that's creating a new reality.

Data security is no longer just an IT responsibility. It's a business responsibility.

A security issue doesn't only affect systems. It can interrupt operations, impact customer trust, create compliance problems, and lead to significant financial consequences.

What's interesting is that many companies still think about security too late.

The focus is often on launching a product, automating a process, or implementing a new system. Security is sometimes viewed as something that can be added later. But once software is in use, addressing security gaps becomes much more difficult and expensive.

Modern software environments are also becoming more complex.

Businesses connect cloud services, third-party platforms, APIs, mobile applications, and internal systems. Every new connection creates additional opportunities, but it can also introduce new risks if not managed properly.

At the same time, customer expectations are changing.

People want to know that their personal information is being handled responsibly. Trust has become an important part of the user experience, and strong security practices play a major role in building that trust.

The challenge for businesses is finding the right balance.

They need systems that are accessible, efficient, and easy to use, while also protecting sensitive information and supporting regulatory requirements.

If there's one thing to take away from today's discussion, it's this:

Data security is no longer just a technical feature of software. It's a fundamental part of how modern businesses operate, grow, and maintain trust in a digital world.

Thanks for listening to the SoftPro Podcast.

If you'd like to discuss your software project, business processes, or digital transformation goals, feel free to reach out through the contact form on our website. Our team will be happy to answer your questions and continue the conversation https://soft-pro.pl/en/contact-us/.

title: What Happens After the MQVP Launch?
author: Net-Devs
description:
Welcome to the Net-Devs Podcast https://net-devs.com/, where we discuss product development, startups, software engineering, and the challenges of building successful digital products.

Today, we're talking about something that many founders don't expect when launching a new product - what happens after the MVP launch?

For many startups, the MVP feels like the finish line.

After months of planning, development, testing, and preparation, the product is finally live. Users can sign up, explore features, and start using what the team has built.

But in reality, launching an MVP is usually the beginning, not the end.

One of the biggest misconceptions in product development is that success comes from building the first version of a product. More often, success comes from what happens next.

Because once real users arrive, assumptions start getting tested.

Some features people love. Others are completely ignored.

Problems that seemed important during planning may turn out to matter very little to users. At the same time, unexpected requests and use cases begin to appear.

This is where many teams face a difficult challenge.

They suddenly have more ideas than resources.

Every piece of feedback feels important. Every feature request sounds valuable. Every customer conversation suggests a different direction.

The risk is trying to build everything at once.

Instead of learning from user behavior, teams start reacting to every request. The product grows, but it doesn't necessarily improve.

Another challenge is scalability.

An MVP is often designed to validate an idea, not support long-term growth. As user numbers increase, teams begin dealing with performance issues, technical debt, infrastructure decisions, and product complexity that weren't priorities during the initial launch.

And then there's the question every founder eventually faces:

What should we build next?

The answer isn't always more features.

Sometimes the most important work happens behind the scenes - improving onboarding, simplifying workflows, fixing usability issues, or understanding why users leave before becoming active customers.

The companies that grow successfully are often the ones that spend more time listening than building.

They treat the MVP as a learning tool rather than a finished product.

Because product-market fit rarely happens on launch day.

It usually comes through multiple iterations, difficult decisions, and a willingness to challenge original assumptions.

If there's one thing to take away from today's discussion, it's this:

An MVP doesn't prove you've built the right product.

It gives you the opportunity to find out whether you're building the right product.

And what happens after launch often matters far more than the launch itself.

Thanks for listening to the Net-Devs Podcast.

If you'd like to continue the conversation about startups, product development, and building digital products, feel free to contact our team via emai contact@net-devs.coml.

title: Christian Intelligence - Taking Prayer to the Next Level!
author: Barry Valleskey
description:
Throughout the Gospels, Jesus did something His disciples couldn't fully understand. He would leave them — sometimes for hours, sometimes through the entire night — and go alone to a quiet, solitary place to pray. On a mountain. In a garden. In the wilderness for forty days. What was He doing in those long hours of silence and solitude? Scripture tells us He emerged transformed, empowered, and perfectly aligned with the Father's will. He walked on water after one of those nights. He chose His twelve after another. He faced the cross after Gethsemane.

We believe Jesus was doing something the modern world is only beginning to understand — He was entering a deep, focused state of communion where the conscious mind grew quiet and the Spirit of God could move freely in His innermost being. Not distracted prayer. Not rushed prayer. Deep prayer. The kind that rewires the soul.

Christian Intelligence was built on this model.

Using the science of hypnosis — the intentional quieting of the conscious mind — we create a sacred window for the Holy Spirit to do His deepest work in you. Not from the outside in, but from the inside out. Just as Paul prayed in Ephesians 3:16 — "that you be strengthened with power through His Spirit in your inner being." Just as God promised in Ezekiel 36:26 — "I will give you a new heart." Just as Romans 12:2 commands — "be transformed by the renewing of your mind."

This is not a shortcut. This is not a trick. This is taking prayer to the next level — the way Jesus modeled it, the way Scripture commands it, and the way the Holy Spirit has always worked: deep in the subconscious, in the quiet places, where no one else can go but you and God.

That is Christian Intelligence

title: Abuse Fifty Years Ago Verses Now!
author: Stephanie Murphy
description: Once upon a time about 50 to 100 years ago there was children who listened to their parents! Sounds like all the families were swell. Usually the mothers were all homemakers and they keep the house clean and a pie cooking for dessert. Children trusted and respected parents and most adults actually. It’s a true story and I am not lying, but those children were also afraid of getting into trouble. At public schools in the 1950’s they didn’t expel the student. That would be a vacation, but instead if they were bad at school, they had what was called, “the strap.” The principal didn’t phone the parents ahead, instead the defiant child was told to hold out there wrist and while the student was shaking, they got whipped with this piece of leather that may have had holes in it. Children were usually so scared if they got the strap, that there parents would find out. If there parents found out then the child would most likely get a spanking and get grounded. Also made to do house chores.

title: Contagions - une histoire des épidémies
author: RTBF
description: Saviez vous que la peste noire a décimé un tiers de la population européenne en à peine cinq ans ? Que le choléra a bouleversé Bruxelles au point de transformer durablement la ville et son réseau sanitaire ? Et que la variole reste la seule maladie infectieuse totalement éradiquée grâce à la vaccination ? Cette série documentaire, remonte la trace des grandes épidémies qui ont marqué l’histoire de l’humanité. De la peste à la lèpre, du choléra à la tuberculose, de l’ergotisme à la syphilis, "Contagions" raconte des maladies qui ont semé la peur, provoqué des paniques collectives, inspiré des croyances, transformé les villes et accéléré les progrès de la médecine. Aux côtés d’historiens, biologistes, anthropologues et archivistes, ce podcast explore les quarantaines médiévales, les processions religieuses, les boucs émissaires, la naissance des vaccins, les grandes politiques d’hygiène publique ou encore les mutations sociales provoquées par les pandémies. À travers des archives, des manuscrits anciens, des récits historiques et des découvertes scientifiques, "Contagions" éclaire notre rapport à la peur, au corps, à la science et à la mort. "Contagions - une histoire des épidémies " à découvrir dès le 22 juin.  Écriture et réalisation : Céline Rase Réalisation sonore : Jonathan Remy Un podcast RTBF La Première, en partenariat avec l’ULB et avec le soutien des joueurs de la Loterie Nationale.  Hébergé par Audiomeans. Visitez audiomeans.fr/politique-de-confidentialite pour plus d'informations.
